Title: Unraveling the contribution of OsSOS2 in conferring salinity and drought tolerance in a high-yielding rice

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsSOS2 studied here corresponds to locus LOC_Os06g40370 from rice and was functionally analyzed only in its native rice background (IR64), not as a heterologous transgene in other species.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. Stable OsSOS2 overexpression (OESOS2) and knockdown (KDSOS2) lines were generated in the high-yielding but stress-sensitive rice cultivar IR64 and molecularly confirmed by PCR, Southern blot, and siRNA detection.
2. Under salinity, OsSOS2-OE plants maintain higher root and shoot growth, higher relative water content, lower electrolyte leakage and MDA, and a higher K+/Na+ ratio than WT and KD plants, indicating improved ionic and osmotic stress tolerance.
3. OsSOS2-OE plants retain more chlorophyll, accumulate more proline, and maintain favorable macro- and micro-element profiles (including K+, P+, Ca2+, Mg2+, and Si) in roots and shoots under salinity compared with WT and KD plants.
4. Microscopy shows OsSOS2 overexpression reduces Na+ accumulation and cell damage in roots, preserves root cortical and vascular bundle cell integrity, and maintains stomatal opening under salinity, whereas KD plants show severe cell damage and stomatal closure.
5. OsSOS2-OE plants exhibit lower H2O2 accumulation and higher SOD, APX, and CAT activities under salinity, linking OsSOS2 to enhanced antioxidant-mediated ROS detoxification and redox homeostasis.
6. OsSOS2 overexpression increases transcript abundance of stress-responsive genes OsHBP1b, OsMYB, OsRR21, OsSOS3, and OsSOS1 under salinity, suggesting OsSOS2 acts within a broader regulatory network for salinity tolerance.
7. At the reproductive stage, OsSOS2-OE lines sustain panicle length, spikelet number, grain number per panicle, and thousand-grain weight much better than WT and KD lines under both salinity and drought, demonstrating improved yield stability under osmotic stresses.
8. The study concludes that OsSOS2 has multifaceted regulatory roles integrating ion, osmotic, and redox homeostasis, making it a promising target for biotechnological improvement of salinity and drought tolerance in rice.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of OsSOS2 enhances salinity and drought tolerance in rice by maintaining growth, ion homeostasis, redox homeostasis, stomatal function, and yield under stress, whereas OsSOS2 knockdown increases sensitivity.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: OsSOS2 overexpression enhances activities of antioxidant enzymes (SOD, APX, CAT) leading to improved ROS scavenging and redox homeostasis; it promotes proline accumulation for osmotic adjustment and maintains higher cytosolic Ca2+ levels that are proposed to activate SOS3 and SOS1 to restore ion homeostasis under salinity.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: SOS (Salt Overly Sensitive) signaling pathway controlling Na+ efflux and ion homeostasis, integrated with antioxidant-mediated redox homeostasis and osmotic stress responses under salinity and drought.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: OsSOS2 -->(activation)--> OsRR21; OsSOS2 overexpression induces higher OsRR21 transcript levels under salinity. OsSOS2 -->(activation)--> OsHBP1b; OsSOS2 -->(activation)--> OsMYB; OsSOS2 overexpression increases OsHBP1b and OsMYB transcript abundance under salinity compared with WT and KD plants.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: OsSOS2 is constitutively expressed in WT and overexpression plants under control and salinity, with higher transcript abundance in OESOS2 lines under salt stress; OsSOS2 transcript is undetectable in KDSOS2 lines under both control and salinity conditions.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High salinity (NaCl-induced and electrical conductivity-based salinization) and cyclic drought stress imposed at seedling, vegetative, and reproductive stages.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Functional characterization of OsSOS2 in regulating ionic, osmotic, and redox homeostasis to improve salinity and drought tolerance and sustain yield in high-yielding rice.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (salinity tolerance, drought tolerance), oxidative stress tolerance, and yield-related traits under stress.
Plant_Genes.Favorable_Allele: OsSOS2 overexpression allele (OESOS2 lines) in IR64 background, which confers higher salinity and drought tolerance and sustains yield compared with WT and OsSOS2 knockdown lines.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 150 mM NaCl for 72 h, OsSOS2-OE seedlings show only ~10% inhibition of root and shoot growth versus ~18–25% inhibition in WT and >25% in KD; RWC decreases by ~18% in OE versus 34.7% in WT and >40% in KD; electrolyte leakage increases by ~25% in OE versus 43.4% in WT and >74% in KD; MDA increases by ~50% in OE versus 64.2% in WT and >69% in KD; K+/Na+ ratio decreases by ~10% in OE versus ~22% in WT and >45.7% in KD. Under salinity at vegetative stage, chlorophyll decreases by ~15% in OE versus 34.5% in WT and ~50% in KD, while proline increases by >60% in OE. At reproductive stage, under salinity, panicle length decreases by 18.1% (OE-15) and 14.1% (OE-19) versus 29.5% in WT and ~50% in KD; spikelets per panicle decrease by 3.4–4.7% in OE versus marked reductions in WT and KD; grain number per panicle decreases by 12.8–20.2% in OE versus much larger reductions in WT and KD; thousand-grain weight decreases by 6.1–10.8% in OE versus 20.7% in WT and ~29–31% in KD. Under drought, OE plants also show smaller reductions in these yield traits (e.g. TGW only 12.4–15.5% decrease).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: OsSOS2-OE plants show reduced Na+ accumulation and cell damage in roots under salinity, better preservation of root cortical and vascular bundle cell integrity, lower H2O2 accumulation in leaves, open stomata under salinity where KD stomata are completely closed, enhanced SOD, APX, and CAT activities under salt stress, and development of macro trichomes on leaves under salinity.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Oryza sativa cv. IR64 wild type (WT); OsSOS2 overexpression lines OESOS2 (e.g. OE-15, OE-19); OsSOS2 knockdown RNAi lines KDSOS2 (e.g. KD-4, KD-7); T2–T4 generations grown in greenhouse/controlled conditions.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Generation of transgenic overexpression and RNAi lines via Agrobacterium tumefaciens (LBA4404); PCR and Southern blot for transgene integration; small RNA blot to detect OsSOS2 siRNA; salinity treatments with 150 mM NaCl on seedlings and 10–12 dS m^-1 ECiw on vegetative and reproductive plants; drought stress by withholding irrigation at reproductive stage; measurements of root/shoot length, relative water content, electrolyte leakage, MDA, K+/Na+ ratio, chlorophyll and proline content; ionome analysis by EDXRF; confocal microscopy with CoroNa Green and propidium iodide; DAB staining for H2O2; H2O2 quantification; SOD, APX, CAT activity assays; SEM of roots and stomata; RNA gel blot for OsSOS2 and stress-related genes; yield and yield component measurements (panicle length, spikelets per panicle, grains per panicle, thousand-grain weight).
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation via constitutive overexpression of OsSOS2 and RNAi-mediated knockdown in rice cv. IR64 followed by comprehensive physiological, biochemical, anatomical, microscopic, molecular, and agronomic analyses under salinity and drought stresses.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Reverse genetics: cloning of full-length OsSOS2 cDNA and generation of overexpression (pCAMBIA1304-OsSOS2) and RNAi knockdown (pFGC1008-OsSOS2) lines in IR64 rice via Agrobacterium-mediated transformation.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: OsSOS2 is a strong candidate gene for engineering and marker-assisted introgression to develop high-yielding rice cultivars with enhanced tolerance to combined salinity and drought stress, maintaining grain yield and quality under adverse conditions and potentially expanding cultivation to salt- and drought-prone areas.
